descriptions.description: The Derby is a medium-range, beyond-visual-range active-radar-homing air-to-air missile developed by Rafael Advanced Defense Systems of Israel. It is a fire-and-forget weapon utilizing continuous-wave radar guidance, enabling multiple simultaneous engagements without requiring the launching aircraft to maintain radar lock. The Derby is larger and longer-range than its sister weapon, the Python-5, serving as the primary beyond-visual-range interceptor for the SPYDER ground-based air defense system and Israeli fighter aircraft. The missile combines proven radar-guidance technology with a high-impulse rocket motor to deliver beyond-visual-range capability to multiple launch platforms.
descriptions.shortDescription: Israeli active-radar-homing beyond-visual-range AAM for fire-and-forget engagements.

descriptions.description: The Derby employs an active-radar seeker for autonomous target acquisition and terminal guidance, allowing launches without sustained illumination from the parent aircraft. The missile is an enlarged derivative of the Python-4 airframe with an active-radar seeker package. It is powered by a solid-fuel rocket motor achieving Mach 4 speeds. The warhead is a high-explosive fragmentation type with 23 kg of explosive material and proximity fuzing. The larger diameter (160 mm) and length (3.62 m) compared to the Python-5 provide increased fuel capacity and extended range. Integration with SPYDER provides networked engagement capability with cooperative fire control across multiple air defense units.
descriptions.shortDescription: Enlarged Python variant with active-radar seeker and extended-range solid-fuel motor.

descriptions.description: The Derby serves as the beyond-visual-range component of the SPYDER integrated air defense system, working in concert with the short-range Python-5. It is operationally employed by Israeli Air Force fighter aircraft including F-15 and F-16 variants equipped with active-radar fire-control systems. The SPYDER ground-based platform, combining both Derby and Python missiles, has been exported to multiple nations for tactical air defense roles. The Derby’s fire-and-forget capability and extended range make it suitable for protecting airspace against stand-off air threats including cruise missiles and aircraft operating at medium altitudes.
descriptions.shortDescription: Primary BVR interceptor for Israeli fighters and SPYDER ground-based air defense system.
